term: Federal Reserve (Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System)
plain_definition: The United States' central bank, and -- defined in the GENIUS Act as 'the Board' -- one of the agencies that can be a 'primary Federal payment stablecoin regulator': the appropriate Federal banking agency (which for a state member bank or the subsidiary of a bank holding company is the Federal Reserve) supervises payment-stablecoin issuance by subsidiaries of insured depository institutions under the Act. The Federal Reserve separately publishes banking, consumer, and regulatory policy press releases covering its supervisory positions on banks' digital-asset activities.
formal_definition: GENIUS Act (Public Law 119-27) section 2(3) (12 U.S.C. 5901(3)) defines 'Board' as the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System; section 2(25) defines 'primary Federal payment stablecoin regulator' to include, for a subsidiary of an insured depository institution other than an insured credit union, that institution's 'appropriate Federal banking agency.' Established by the Federal Reserve Act of 1913. Press releases: federalreserve.gov/feeds/press_bcreg.xml (per this radar's watcher configuration).
